monday.question: Which sentence uses 'ought to' correctly to give formal advice?
options: You ought to apologizing for what you said.
You ought to apologize for what you said.
You ought apologize for what you said.
You ought to apologized for what you said.
monday.correctAnswer: You ought to apologize for what you said.

monday.question: Complete: 'How about _____ (go) for a walk? Fresh air might help you feel better.'
Complete: 'If I _____ you, I would speak to your boss directly.' (Use the correct form of 'be' — subjunctive mood)
Express this idea in correct English using the noun 'advice': 'I want to offer someone a helpful suggestion.' (Remember: 'advice' is uncountable.)
monday.correctAnswer: give advice|give some advice|give a piece of advice|offer advice

monday.question: Your friend has a flight in 30 minutes and hasn't left yet. Which advice is most appropriate?
options: You should leave soon.
You could leave now if you want.
You'd better leave right now or you'll miss your flight.
Why don't you leave later?
monday.correctAnswer: You'd better leave right now or you'll miss your flight.

monday.question: Complete: 'You _____ not stay up so late every night — your health is seriously suffering.' (Use the modal that implies a strong warning with consequences)
Complete: 'Why don't you _____ (take) a break? You've been working for five hours straight.' (Write the correct verb form after 'Why don't you')
A student wrote: 'She gave me an advice about my career.' This sentence has a mistake. Write the corrected version.
monday.correctAnswer: She gave me advice about my career.|She gave me some advice about my career.|She gave me a piece of advice about my career.

monday.question: Which sentence about 'advice' is grammatically correct?
options: He gave me an advice about the situation.
He gave me advices about the situation.
He gave me some advice about the situation.
He gave me a lot of advices about the situation.
monday.correctAnswer: He gave me some advice about the situation.

monday.question: Complete: 'What about _____ (see) a financial advisor? Your debt situation sounds really serious.'
Complete: 'You had better _____ late to the interview — first impressions matter a lot.' (Write the two-word negative form: 'not be')
How do you correctly say 'I suggest that she study more'? (Focus on the correct verb form after 'that' in a suggest-clause.)
monday.correctAnswer: I suggest that she study more.|I suggest that she studies more.

monday.question: Which sentence expresses a GENTLE suggestion rather than a strong warning?
options: You'd better see a doctor immediately.
You should definitely see a doctor.
You could try drinking more water — it sometimes helps.
You ought to see a doctor right away.
monday.correctAnswer: You could try drinking more water — it sometimes helps.

monday.question: Complete: 'I recommend _____ (read) this book — it has excellent tips for managing work-related stress.'
Complete: 'If I were in your position, I _____ (not/accept) that offer without negotiating the salary first.' (Write the contracted negative form + verb)
Use the collocation 'seek advice' in a sentence about someone facing a difficult legal situation.
monday.correctAnswer: You should seek legal advice.|He decided to seek advice from a lawyer.|I recommend that you seek professional advice.|She needs to seek advice immediately.

monday.question: Which sentence uses 'suggest' correctly?
options: I suggest you to talk to your parents about this.
I suggest you talking to your parents about this.
I suggest that you talk to your parents about this.
I suggest to you that you to talk to your parents.
monday.correctAnswer: I suggest that you talk to your parents about this.

monday.question: Complete: 'If I were in your _____, I'd start looking for a new job immediately.' (Use the idiom body part meaning 'situation')
Complete: '_____ if you talked to her directly? Maybe she doesn't know how you feel.' (Write the two-word expression to start this suggestion)
Which word is the VERB form of the noun 'advice'?
options: advisable
advisory
advise
advisement

tuesday.question: Which modal verb is generally considered more FORMAL when giving advice?
options: could
should
ought to
had better

tuesday.question: Complete: 'You _____ exercise regularly if you want to improve your energy levels and overall health.' (Use the modal for general advice)
Complete: 'How about _____ (try) meditation? Many people find it very helpful for managing stress.' (Write the correct verb form after 'How about')
How do you express 'seguir o conselho de alguém' in English? Use a common English collocation with the verb 'take'.
tuesday.correctAnswer: take someone's advice|take advice|follow someone's advice|take the advice

tuesday.question: Which is the correct structure for 'Why don't you' + verb?
options: Why don't you to apply for the position?
Why don't you applying for the position?
Why don't you apply for the position?
Why don't you applied for the position?
tuesday.correctAnswer: Why don't you apply for the position?

tuesday.question: Complete: 'You'd _____ see a doctor — that cough has lasted more than two weeks now.'
Complete the second conditional advice: 'If I were you, I _____ not ignore those warning signs.' (Write the correct modal)
A student asked: 'Can you give me an advice?' This sentence has an error. Write the correct version of the question.
tuesday.correctAnswer: Can you give me some advice?|Can you give me a piece of advice?|Could you give me some advice?|Could you give me advice?

tuesday.question: What is the correct structure after 'What about' to make a suggestion?
options: What about you call him and explain?
What about calling him and explaining?
What about to call him and explain?
What about call him to explain?
tuesday.correctAnswer: What about calling him and explaining?

tuesday.question: Complete: 'You _____ try talking to a counselor — sometimes an outside perspective really helps.' (Use the modal for a gentle, non-pressuring suggestion)
Complete: 'I recommend that you _____ (get) your blood pressure checked at least once a year.' (Write the correct verb form in the that-clause)
Use the collocation 'follow a recommendation' in a sentence. For example, about a patient who does what their doctor suggested.
tuesday.correctAnswer: She followed the doctor's recommendation.|He decided to follow the recommendation.|I always follow my doctor's recommendations.|follow a recommendation

tuesday.question: In the second conditional for giving advice, which form of 'be' is correct?
options: If I was you, I would reconsider the offer.
If I were you, I would reconsider the offer.
If I am you, I would reconsider the offer.
If I be you, I would reconsider the offer.
tuesday.correctAnswer: If I were you, I would reconsider the offer.

tuesday.question: Complete: 'You ought _____ apologize to her — what you said was really hurtful.' (Write the preposition that follows 'ought')
Complete: 'I suggest _____ (carry) an umbrella today — the forecast says heavy rain.' (Write the correct verb form after 'suggest')
Use the phrase 'ask for an opinion' in a complete sentence. For example, asking a trusted friend what they think about an important decision.
tuesday.correctAnswer: I'll ask for her opinion.|Could I ask for your opinion?|He asked for my opinion about the plan.|ask for an opinion

tuesday.question: Which sentence uses 'had better not' correctly?
options: You had not better park there — it's a fire lane.
You better had not park there — it's a fire lane.
You had better not park there — it's a fire lane.
You had better to not park there — it's a fire lane.
tuesday.correctAnswer: You had better not park there — it's a fire lane.

tuesday.question: Complete: 'What _____ you asked your supervisor for more time on the project?' (Use the word that turns this into a suggestion)
Complete: 'You _____ post personal information online — it can be very dangerous.' (Write the contracted negative form of 'should')
'Had better' implies urgency and a possible consequence. In which situation is 'had better' clearly more appropriate than 'should'?
options: You had better drink more water — it's generally healthy.
You had better call an ambulance — he's unconscious and not breathing.
You had better consider joining a gym at some point.
You had better try to read more books in general.
tuesday.correctAnswer: You had better call an ambulance — he's unconscious and not breathing.

wednesday.question: Which sentence uses 'Why doesn't he' correctly to make a suggestion?
options: Why doesn't he to look for a new apartment?
Why doesn't he looking for a new apartment?
Why doesn't he look for a new apartment?
Why doesn't he looks for a new apartment?
wednesday.correctAnswer: Why doesn't he look for a new apartment?

wednesday.question: Complete: 'If I were in your position, I _____ hire a financial advisor to help manage the debt.' (Write the modal verb)
wednesday.correctAnswer: would

wednesday.question: Correct this sentence: 'She gave me an advice about my relationship problems.' Write the fully corrected version.
wednesday.correctAnswer: She gave me advice about my relationship problems.|She gave me some advice about my relationship problems.|She gave me a piece of advice about my relationship problems.

wednesday.question: How do you use the idiom 'in your shoes' to give advice? Write a complete sentence starting with 'If I were in your shoes...'
wednesday.correctAnswer: If I were in your shoes, I would reconsider.|If I were in your shoes, I'd talk to her directly.|If I were in your shoes, I wouldn't give up.|If I were in your shoes, I would ask for help.

wednesday.question: Which sentence uses 'suggest' with the correct structure?
options: I suggest to take a day off — you look exhausted.
I suggest taking a day off — you look exhausted.
I suggest you to take a day off — you look exhausted.
I suggest took a day off — you look exhausted.
wednesday.correctAnswer: I suggest taking a day off — you look exhausted.

wednesday.question: Complete: 'You'd better _____ (finish) that report before your boss arrives — she's very strict about deadlines.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: finish

wednesday.question: Complete: 'How about _____ (join) a support group? Talking to people in the same situation can be very helpful.' (Write the correct form of the verb)
wednesday.correctAnswer: joining

wednesday.question: Your friend is overwhelmed by work pressure. Write advice using 'should' and the phrase 'set boundaries'.
wednesday.correctAnswer: You should set boundaries at work.|She should set boundaries with her colleagues.|You should learn to set boundaries.|He should set some boundaries.

wednesday.question: Which sentence uses 'recommend' with the correct structure?
options: I recommend you to see a specialist about this.
I recommend you seeing a specialist about this.
I recommend seeing a specialist about this.
I recommend to see a specialist about this.
wednesday.correctAnswer: I recommend seeing a specialist about this.

wednesday.question: Complete: 'You _____ not procrastinate — the earlier you start, the less stressed you'll feel.' (Use the modal for general advice)
wednesday.correctAnswer: should

wednesday.question: Complete: 'What about _____ (ask) your landlord for an extension on the rent payment?' (Write the correct verb form)
wednesday.correctAnswer: asking

wednesday.question: How do you give a formal suggestion using 'propose'? Write a sentence proposing that someone take a well-deserved vacation.
wednesday.correctAnswer: I propose that you take a vacation.|I propose taking a vacation.|I'd like to propose that she takes a vacation.|I propose that we all take a break.

wednesday.question: Which sentence correctly uses the second conditional to give advice?
options: If I were you, I will look for a second opinion.
If I was you, I would look for a second opinion.
If I were you, I would look for a second opinion.
If I were you, I will be looking for a second opinion.
wednesday.correctAnswer: If I were you, I would look for a second opinion.

wednesday.question: Complete: 'Why don't you _____ (speak) to a therapist? Talking about your feelings can really make a difference.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: speak

wednesday.question: Complete: 'You ought _____ start saving money now — retirement will come sooner than you think.' (Write the missing word between 'ought' and 'start')
wednesday.correctAnswer: to

wednesday.question: Use 'had better' to give a strong warning about procrastination — tell someone they need to stop putting off their work immediately.
wednesday.correctAnswer: You'd better stop procrastinating.|You had better stop putting off your work.|You'd better start working now or you'll miss the deadline.|You'd better not keep delaying.

wednesday.question: Which sentence correctly uses a collocation with the noun 'advice'?
options: She decided to follow an advice from her mentor.
She decided to take the advice from her mentor.
She decided to make an advice with her mentor.
She decided to do an advice from her mentor.
wednesday.correctAnswer: She decided to take the advice from her mentor.

wednesday.question: Complete: 'You _____ try taking a different route to work — it might save you a lot of time.' (Use the modal for a gentle suggestion)
wednesday.correctAnswer: could

wednesday.question: Complete the negative second conditional: 'If I were you, I _____ sign that contract without reading every clause carefully.' (Write the contracted negative modal + verb)
wednesday.correctAnswer: wouldn't sign|would not sign

wednesday.question: Which discourse marker is most appropriate for introducing the SINGLE most important piece of advice?
options: However
Nevertheless
Above all
On the other hand
wednesday.correctAnswer: Above all

thursday.question: Your colleague hasn't slept properly in two days and is about to operate heavy machinery. Which advice is most appropriate?
options: You could maybe rest a little bit.
You should probably sleep at some point today.
You'd better get some rest before you cause a serious accident.
Why don't you consider sleeping eventually?
thursday.correctAnswer: You'd better get some rest before you cause a serious accident.

thursday.question: Complete: 'I suggest that she _____ (not/ignore) the symptoms any longer — they could indicate something serious.'
Complete: 'Why don't you _____ (ask) your professor for an extension? You have a valid reason.' (Write only the base verb)
Use the adjective 'advisable' correctly in a sentence warning someone about the risks of driving when exhausted.
thursday.correctAnswer: It is not advisable to drive when you are exhausted.|It is advisable to rest before driving.|Driving when tired is not advisable.

thursday.question: Which sentence uses 'What if' correctly to introduce a suggestion?
options: What if to try a completely different approach?
What if trying a different approach altogether?
What if you tried a completely different approach?
What if you to try a different approach entirely?
thursday.correctAnswer: What if you tried a completely different approach?

thursday.question: Complete: 'If I were in your _____, I would take the promotion — it's a fantastic opportunity.' (Use the idiom meaning 'in your situation')
Complete: 'I recommend _____ (cut) down on caffeine if you're having trouble sleeping at night.' (Write the correct verb form)
Write a complete sentence using the expression 'seek professional advice' for someone facing a complicated legal issue.
thursday.correctAnswer: You should seek professional advice.|He needs to seek professional advice from a lawyer.|I strongly recommend that you seek professional advice.|You ought to seek professional advice immediately.

thursday.question: Which word correctly completes: 'She _____ the advice of a financial expert before making any major investments.'?
options: asked to
turned for
sought
demanded

thursday.question: Complete: 'You ought to _____ more careful with your spending — you're running very low on savings.' (Write the base form of 'be')
Complete: 'You had better _____ to back up your files before running the system update.' (Write the two-word negative form)
Use 'ought to' formally to advise someone about a challenging relationship. Write a complete, natural-sounding sentence.
thursday.correctAnswer: You ought to communicate more openly with your partner.|She ought to set clear boundaries in the relationship.|He ought to seek couples counseling.|You ought to address these issues before they get worse.

thursday.question: What is true about the difference between 'How about' and 'What about' in making suggestions?
options: 'How about' takes a base verb; 'What about' takes a gerund.
They have the same meaning and both require a gerund or noun.
'How about' is formal; 'What about' is informal.
'What about' requires a full clause; 'How about' does not.
thursday.correctAnswer: They have the same meaning and both require a gerund or noun.

thursday.question: Complete: 'You _____ eat more vegetables — your diet is far too high in processed food.' (Use the modal for general, non-urgent advice)
Complete: 'You _____ try journaling to help manage your anxiety — it works for a lot of people.' (Use the modal for a gentle suggestion with no pressure)
Use 'most importantly' as a discourse marker to introduce the key piece of advice about managing work stress.
thursday.correctAnswer: Most importantly, make sure you get enough sleep.|Most importantly, you should take regular breaks throughout the day.|Most importantly, don't ignore the early signs of burnout.|Most importantly, talk to someone you trust.

thursday.question: What is the NOUN form of the verb 'suggest'?
options: suggestment
suggestive
suggestion
suggestance

thursday.question: Complete: 'If I were you, I _____ apply for that scholarship — you have an outstanding academic record.' (Write the full modal + base verb)
Complete: 'Why _____ she join a language exchange program? It would really improve her speaking skills.' (Write the one word needed to complete 'Why doesn't')
Which sentence correctly uses 'above all' in an advice context?
options: Above all, you should exercising regularly and eating well.
Above all, make sure you get enough rest and take care of yourself.
Above all try to sleep but not really that much.
Above all you had better to rest and exercise.
thursday.correctAnswer: Above all, make sure you get enough rest and take care of yourself.

friday.question: A friend says: 'I'm really overwhelmed by my exams.' Which response is most natural and grammatically correct?
options: You had better to study harder immediately.
Why don't you making a study plan and sticking to it?
How about creating a study schedule and taking short breaks between sessions?
You ought studying more and relaxing less.
friday.correctAnswer: How about creating a study schedule and taking short breaks between sessions?

friday.question: Complete: 'I strongly _____ (recommend) that you see a specialist before making any decisions about the surgery.'
Complete the formal advice: 'You ought to _____ a lawyer before signing any legal agreement.' (Write the base verb meaning 'to speak with professionally')
Write a sentence using 'first of all' as a discourse marker combined with a modal verb to give structured advice to someone starting a new job.
friday.correctAnswer: First of all, you should introduce yourself to your colleagues.|First of all, you ought to learn the company's policies.|First of all, you could observe how things work before making changes.|First of all, you should ask your manager about your main responsibilities.

friday.question: Identify the sentence that contains a grammatical error:
options: You should get more sleep — it affects everything.
If I were you, I would reconsider that decision.
How about to go for a run this evening?
You could try a completely different approach.
friday.correctAnswer: How about to go for a run this evening?

friday.question: Complete: 'It is extremely _____ to drive when you're severely sleep-deprived — many fatal accidents happen this way.' (Choose: 'beneficial', 'advisable', or 'risky')
Complete the second conditional advice: 'If I were you, I _____ look for a therapist to talk about these deeper issues.' (Write the full modal + base verb)
Use 'follow a recommendation' in a complete sentence about a patient who does exactly what their doctor advised.
friday.correctAnswer: She followed the doctor's recommendation.|He decided to follow the recommendation and rest for a week.|I always follow my doctor's recommendations.|The patient followed the recommendation and recovered quickly.

friday.question: You're in a formal business meeting and want to propose an idea tactfully. Which is the most appropriate expression?
options: Why don't you guys just try doing it differently?
You'd better try a completely different approach.
I would like to suggest that we reconsider our current strategy.
How about totally overhauling everything we've done?
friday.correctAnswer: I would like to suggest that we reconsider our current strategy.

friday.question: Complete: '_____ about taking a few days off? You clearly need to recharge your energy.' (Write the word that begins this suggestion structure)
Complete: 'You _____ better not ignore those chest pains — please call a doctor right away.' (Write the contracted form of 'had')
Write a recommendation for someone who wants to improve their English fluency. Use 'recommend' followed by a gerund.
friday.correctAnswer: I recommend watching English movies.|I recommend practicing every single day.|I recommend reading books in English.|I recommend speaking with native speakers as often as possible.

friday.question: What is the correct NOUN form of the verb 'recommend'?
options: recommendment
recommendation
recommendance
recommendal

friday.question: Complete: 'You'd better _____ (save) your work frequently — the system has been crashing without warning lately.'
Complete: 'I suggest that everyone _____ at least 15 minutes before the meeting begins.' (Write the correct verb form of 'arrive' in the that-clause)
Use 'in addition' as a discourse marker to add a second piece of advice after: 'You should exercise more.' Write the follow-up sentence.
friday.correctAnswer: In addition, you should improve your diet.|In addition, you could try to sleep earlier.|In addition, you ought to reduce your caffeine intake.|In addition, you should drink more water throughout the day.

friday.question: Which sentence about the second conditional advice structure is INCORRECT?
options: If I were you, I'd apologize to her immediately.
If I were you, I would ask for professional help.
If I were you, I will reconsider that risky decision.
If I were you, I wouldn't take that unnecessary risk.
friday.correctAnswer: If I were you, I will reconsider that risky decision.

friday.question: Complete: 'You _____ try setting a daily routine — it would really help with your procrastination problem.' (Use the modal for a gentle, optional suggestion)
Complete: 'What about _____ (switch) to a plant-based diet? There is strong evidence that it improves long-term health.' (Write the correct verb form)
Which sentence correctly uses 'besides' as a discourse marker in an advice context?
options: Besides, you should also take care of your mental health, not just your physical health.
Besides you should considering your mental health too.
Besides, you should to consider your mental health as well.
Besides to that, you should consider your mental well-being.
friday.correctAnswer: Besides, you should also take care of your mental health, not just your physical health.
